executive_summary: Today's idiosyncratic moves reveal three distinct causal clusters with varying coherence. Cluster 1 (consumer staples/healthcare, coherence 0.50) shows the most severe systematic repricing: CNC collapsed -20.9% (6.5σ) while packaged foods CPB, CAG, and GIS suffered -15.7% to -11.8% moves at extreme 7.4-9.3 sigma levels with tight causal links (p=0.69-0.75). Cluster 0 (technology, coherence 0.24) is bifurcated with SNDK +26.2% epicenter versus ULTA -16.8% (7.3σ), PSKY -18.2% (5.0σ), and ADBE -11.3% (5.7σ) weakness, suggesting diffuse rather than unified causation. Cluster 2 (materials, coherence 0.56) shows the tightest structure with MOS anchoring fertilizer weakness propagating to CF and DOW. The packaged food collapse and CNC managed care crisis demand immediate investigation, while the technology cluster's low coherence suggests multiple independent catalysts rather than a single shared driver.

alerts.title: ULTA -16.8% Idiosyncratic Drop Anchors 22-Stock Technology/Consumer Discretionary Cluster
alerts.summary: ULTA experienced a -16.8% idiosyncratic move (7.3 sigma) that shows causal linkage to ADBE (-11.3%, 5.7 sigma) and COO (-8.2%, 5.3 sigma) with p-values of 0.36 and 0.44 respectively. This cluster of 22 stocks spans technology and consumer discretionary sectors with coherence 0.24, suggesting a shared but diffuse driver. The epicenter is actually SNDK (+26.2% idio), creating a bifurcated cluster with extreme positive and negative residuals. Without specific news catalysts provided, the causal mechanism linking beauty retail (ULTA) to enterprise software (ADBE, WDAY, FISV), healthcare tech (RMD, TMO), and memory (MU, WDC, SNDK) remains unclear, though the SNDK surge may indicate sector-specific storage/memory news affecting tech spending expectations.

alerts.title: CPB -15.7% Idiosyncratic Drop (8.1 Sigma) Shows Strong Causal Links to CAG, GIS
alerts.summary: Campbell Soup (CPB) declined -15.7% on an idiosyncratic basis at 8.1 sigma, demonstrating strong causal relationships with CAG (p=0.69) and GIS (p=0.75). This represents one of the highest-sigma events in the dataset and suggests a packaged food sector-specific catalyst beyond general consumer staples factors. The tight causal linkage indicates potential concerns about pricing power, volume trends, private label competition, or input costs specific to shelf-stable packaged foods. CPB also shows weaker links to SYK (p=0.31) and BA (p=0.36), though the mechanism is unclear.

alerts.title: GIS -11.8% Idiosyncratic Drop at 9.3 Sigma - Highest Sigma Event in Dataset
alerts.summary: General Mills (GIS) declined -11.8% on an idiosyncratic basis at 9.3 sigma, representing the highest sigma event across all flagged moves. The strong causal link from CPB (p=0.75) is the tightest relationship in the entire dataset, indicating GIS is highly responsive to the same driver affecting CPB. Despite a smaller percentage move than CPB or CAG, the 9.3 sigma reading suggests this move is extraordinarily unusual relative to GIS's historical volatility profile, potentially indicating a liquidity event or options-related activity amplifying the fundamental driver.

The high coherence and tight causal structure indicate a genuine materials sector event, potentially related to fertilizer pricing, crop planting expectations, or agricultural commodity trends.

sector_summary.Consumer Staples - Packaged Foods: Catastrophic idiosyncratic repricing with CPB -15.7% (8.1σ), CAG -13.8% (7.4σ), and GIS -11.8% (9.3σ) showing the tightest causal structure in the dataset (p=0.69-0.75). The 9.3σ GIS move is the highest sigma event across all flagged moves. High cluster coherence (0.50) confirms genuine shared catalyst affecting shelf-stable food companies. MKC -10.2% (5.6σ) and KHC -8.0% (5.2σ) extend the weakness. This represents systematic repricing beyond normal sector factors.
sector_summary.Healthcare - Managed Care & Devices: CNC -20.9% (6.5σ) serves as Cluster 1 epicenter, representing the largest percentage idiosyncratic move in the dataset. SYK -7.4% (5.6σ) and PODD -6.1% (3.0σ) show correlated weakness with causal links to packaged foods, suggesting potential shared consumer spending or government reimbursement concerns. The healthcare-staples linkage is unusual but coherence of 0.50 supports genuine connection.
sector_summary.Technology - Software & Services: Bifurcated cluster with low coherence (0.24) indicating multiple drivers. Severe weakness in PSKY -18.2% (5.0σ), ADBE -11.3% (5.7σ), GDDY -12.8% (5.2σ), WDAY, FISV suggests enterprise software/SMB spending concerns. ADBE shows causal centrality with links to COO (p=0.40), BR (p=0.46), ADP (p=0.52). Offset by SNDK +26.2% epicenter with MU, WDC suggesting memory/storage catalyst. Low coherence warrants skepticism about unified narrative.
sector_summary.Consumer Discretionary - Retail: ULTA -16.8% (7.3σ) represents the second-largest percentage idiosyncratic move and shows unusual causal links to technology names. DG -9.8% (4.2σ) and BR -9.2% (5.8σ) extend retail weakness. The ULTA-ADBE connection (p=0.36) is mechanistically unclear given sector divergence, suggesting either spurious correlation or a deeper consumer spending signal affecting both beauty retail and enterprise software.
sector_summary.Industrials: CPRT -9.2% (5.8σ), COO -8.2% (5.3σ), OTIS -6.7% (5.7σ), and BA -8.8% (4.8σ) show synchronized weakness within Cluster 0 and 1. COO receives causal influence from both ULTA (p=0.44) and ADBE (p=0.40). BA's presence in the consumer staples cluster (Cluster 1) with causal link from CPB (p=0.36) is mechanistically unclear.
sector_summary.Materials - Fertilizers & Chemicals: MOS -6.2% (2.2σ) anchors the highest coherence cluster (0.56) with strong causal propagation to CF (p=0.66) and DOW (p=0.53). Despite lower sigma values than other clusters, the tight causal structure and high coherence make this the most reliable signal for systematic fertilizer/agricultural chemical repricing.
